text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously firming. The real driver is the U.S.-Iran ceasefire and Hormuz reopening — that kills the oil-spike fear that was overhanging risk assets this morning. For tech and chip names, that's a quiet tailwind, but yields are still in wait-and-see mode ahead of jobs data, so don't expect a rip.

Your holdings: Three need your eyes. AVGO is your largest and you're down 16% — on margin that's a real hole, and it's sitting closer to the $336 stop than you want. PLTR is down 13% and flagged SELL — momentum has clearly broken. SPAL is down 17% and it's a 2x leveraged ETF, meaning decay eats you alive if it chops sideways. Margin amplifies every one of these.

Watch: 1) Friday jobs number — a hot print could spike yields and hammer growth/tech names further. 2) AVGO price action around $336 stop — if it cracks, your largest position is in freefall.

Bottom line: Your SELL flags are serious — AVGO and PLTR need a hard decision before the jobs report, not after.
